title: cuspidia cernua subsp. cernua
intro: Cuspidia cernua subsp. cernua, also known as the American snakeroot, is a plant species native to North America, characterized by its tall stature and clusters of small white flowers. The medicinal properties of Cuspidia cernua subsp. cernua lie in its roots, which contain the compound podophyllotoxin, a lignan known for its anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and as a treatment for snakebites, and its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system. Cuspidia cernua subsp. cernua is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its roots can be dried and made into a powder for use in herbal remedies.
